items.title: Selangor Expects Up To 25% Medical Cost Hike
items.summary: Selangor MB Amirudin Shari expects medical costs to increase by 15% to 25% amid the Persian Gulf conflict, noting that nearly 70% to 80% of medicines for Malaysia are sourced from overseas. The post Selangor Expects Up To 25% Medical Cost Hike appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: No Look-Back, No Way Forward? Why Malaysia’s Insurance Reform Still Leaves HIV Behind — Mohamad Zakifikri Mohamad Nor
items.summary: The “no look-back” clause asks insurers to stop looking into the past. But if HIV remains excluded, Malaysia’s insurance system is still looking away from the future. The post No Look-Back, No Way Forward? Why Malaysia’s Insurance Reform Still Leaves HIV Behind — Mohamad Zakifikri Mohamad Nor appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH Probe Clears UMMC Over Cancer ‘Cluster’: Mosti
items.summary: Mosti says a probe by MOH's medical radiation regulatory division didn't find radiation safety breaches that could be linked to an alleged cancer cluster at UMMC. The regulator of radiation for medical purposes is the Health DG. The post MOH Probe Clears UMMC Over Cancer ‘Cluster’: Mosti appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Only 15% Of Malaysian Seniors Ageing Well, Dementia Rate Up: NHMS 2025
items.summary: NHMS 2025 reveals a poor state of health among Malaysian seniors: only 15% ageing well, dementia prevalence rose to 10%, and high prevalence of NCDs (diabetes: 39%, hypertension: 73%, high cholesterol: 76%). About 68% live with at least 2 chronic diseases. The post Only 15% Of Malaysian Seniors Ageing Well, Dementia Rate Up: NHMS 2025 appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Corporate Benefit Schemes Can’t Override Doctors’ Clinical Decisions, Says MMA
items.summary: MMA says corporate benefit schemes cannot interfere with doctors' clinical decisions, stressing there's a clear boundary between what's covered by employers and what's prescribed by doctors based on clinical needs. MMA urges MOH to issue clear guidance. The post Corporate Benefit Schemes Can’t Override Doctors’ Clinical Decisions, Says MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: NHMS 2025 Highlights Urgent Need For Dementia Action — DAMAI, ADFM, JOBADA, Dementia Society Perak & Penang Dementia Association
items.summary: We are deeply concerned by the finding that 10% of Malaysians aged 60 years and above are now living with dementia. This represents a significant increase from the 8.5% prevalence of probable dementia among older persons recorded in NHMS 2018. The post NHMS 2025 Highlights Urgent Need For Dementia Action — DAMAI, ADFM, JOBADA, Dementia Society Perak & Penang Dementia Association appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: A Profession Built On Sacrifice Can’t Survive On Sacrifice Alone — Doctor
items.summary: The question is not whether doctors love their country enough; the question is whether the system allows them to stay. Doctors in the public health care system face long hours, heavy workloads, and limited job security (particularly for contract officers). The post A Profession Built On Sacrifice Can’t Survive On Sacrifice Alone — Doctor appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Perodua Halts Popular Diabetes Injection Prescriptions For Staff
items.summary: Perodua Manufacturing directs panel clinics to stop prescribing GLP-1 and GIP/GLP-1 shots for diabetes to staff, pending further evaluation, and to prescribe other oral hypoglycaemic drugs instead. GLP-1 and GIP/GLP-1 meds treat both diabetes and obesity. The post Perodua Halts Popular Diabetes Injection Prescriptions For Staff appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia Makes Deal With China To Stabilise Medical Devices’ Supply
items.summary: Malaysia's MDA has reached an agreement with China to increase resin and naphtha supplies for the manufacture of medical devices in Malaysia, says MOH in admitting the "direct impact" of the global energy crisis on availability of critical medical devices. The post Malaysia Makes Deal With China To Stabilise Medical Devices’ Supply appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: UMMC Denies Radiation Exposure Caused Staff Cancer ‘Cluster’
items.summary: UMMC denies claims that radiation safety violations caused a cancer "cluster" in its radiology department. Three staff were diagnosed with cancer, four with thyroid diseases. The hospital says staff radiation exposure levels were below prescribed limits. The post UMMC Denies Radiation Exposure Caused Staff Cancer ‘Cluster’ appeared first on CodeBlue .
